title: Upon A Physician.
content:
Thou cam'st to cure me, doctor, of my cold,
    And caught'st thyself the more by twenty fold:
    Prithee go home; and for thy credit be
    First cured thyself, then come and cure me.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "Upon A Physician.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
